title: List Comprehensions: One-Line Loops and Filtering
content:
### Day 24: List Comprehensions: One-Line Loops and Filtering

**List Comprehensions** create lists in one line. Cleaner and faster than loops.

**Syntax:** `[expression for item in iterable if condition]`

**Also:** Dict comprehensions `{k:v for ...}`, Set comprehensions `{x for ...}`
codeExample:
# Day 24: List Comprehensions: One-Line Loops and Filtering
# List Comprehensions

# Basic
squares = [x**2 for x in range(1, 11)]
print(f"Squares: {squares}")

# With filter
evens = [x for x in range(20) if x % 2 == 0]
print(f"Evens: {evens}")

# String processing
names = ["alice", "bob", "carol"]
upper = [n.capitalize() for n in names]
print(f"Capitalized: {upper}")

# Nested
matrix = [[i*j for j in range(1,4)] for i in range(1,4)]
print(f"Matrix: {matrix}")

# Dict comprehension
word = "hello"
freq = {c: word.count(c) for c in set(word)}
print(f"Freq: {freq}")

# If-else in comprehension
nums = [1, -2, 3, -4, 5]
abs_nums = [n if n >= 0 else -n for n in nums]
print(f"Absolute: {abs_nums}")
practiceQuestions: Convert a for loop to list comprehension.
keyTakeaways: List Comprehensions create lists in one line.
